admission_type: open access (free access subject to a mandatory verification of personal preparation)

academic_requirements.summary: Free-access (non-restricted) master's programme. Admission requires a recognised Bachelor's degree plus the curricular subject credits below, and a mandatory verification of personal preparation. There is no selection ranking; candidates either meet a grade-based automatic exemption or must pass a pass/fail readiness test.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A Bachelor's degree of at least 180 CFU/ECTS obtained at an Italian university, or an equivalent foreign qualification recognised as suitable.
academic_requirements.required_prior_credits: At least 36 CFU/ECTS (or equivalent knowledge) in the basic-activities scientific-disciplinary sectors (SSD) of bachelor classes L-7, L-8 and L-9, plus at least 45 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in the characterising-activities SSD of classes L-7, L-8 and L-9. All Engineering bachelor's degrees provided by Italian universities are stated to meet the curricular requirements.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Adequate knowledge of fundamental notions in basic subjects (e.g. mathematics and physics) as well as applied and professional engineering topics; the committee also considers the curriculum of the three-year degree.
academic_requirements.gpa_threshold: Candidates whose Bachelor's degree final mark is at least 9/10 of the maximum (or corresponds to ECTS grade 'A' or 'B') are automatically deemed adequate and are exempted from the readiness test.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad with a foreign qualification apply through the UniGeApply (Apply UniGe) portal for pre-evaluation, then complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and obtain a study visa via the Italian Embassy/Consulate.

accepted_proofs: A recognised English-language certificate at level B2 or higher among those accepted by the University (listed on the CLAT page, https://clat.unige.it/en/CertificazioniRiconosciute).
The B2 test offered by the University Language Centre (CLAT).
A degree taught entirely in English, certified officially.
exemption_rules: Holding a recognised B2-or-higher English certificate (or an officially certified English-medium degree) exempts the candidate from English-language verification.
Candidates without an accepted proof must have their English knowledge verified during the personal-preparation test.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English proficiency is verified at the personal-preparation (readiness) stage; candidates without an accepted certificate are assessed for English during the readiness test.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of personal preparation (assessment of adequacy of personal readiness) - a departmental written test or public interview. NOT a TOLC.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants who do not qualify for the grade-based automatic exemption (Bachelor's final mark below 9/10 of the maximum / below ECTS 'A'-'B').
entry_exam_or_test.format: Written test or public interview before a commission of faculty members, assessing general preparation in basic subjects (e.g. mathematics, physics) and applied/professional engineering topics. English knowledge is also checked here for candidates without an accepted B2 proof.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Five scheduled sessions for A.Y. 2026/2027: 8 September 2026, 28 September 2026, 18 November 2026, 18 January 2027, and 26 February 2027.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Outcome is recorded as 'passed' or 'failed' only; there is no numerical ranking. A pass (or the grade-based exemption) is required for enrolment.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
Pre-registration (master's) on the UniGe portal: from 9 April to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment).
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation of enrolment from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment). Enrolment in years after the first runs 15 July to 5 October 2026, with arrears accepted 6 October to 5 November 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: This is a free-access (open) programme, so EU/resident applicants are not ranked; the personal-preparation test is pass/fail and does not gate via a ranking. The programme deadlines page lists the generic UniGe enrolment calendar; the institution-wide spring UniGeApply window applies to non-EU residents in Italy.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After receiving eligibility via UniGeApply, visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and then apply for the study visa at the Italian Embassy/Consulate.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published for this programme. The known fall-session early closures for A.Y. 2026/27 (Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology 13 Feb 2026, Maritime Science and Technology 18 Feb 2026, Computer Engineering bachelor 8 Mar 2026) do NOT include Safe Transport and Logistics Engineering.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session; the UniGeApply fall window for A.Y. 2026/27 (closed 20 March 2026) has already passed as of the research date (10 June 2026).

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport (non-EU) or national identity card (EU)
required_documents.notes: Passport is the only document accepted for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply; EU applicants may use a national identity card.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree certificate (final or provisional) and transcript of records
required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply for pre-evaluation of the foreign qualification and curricular requirements.
Listed among UniGeApply submissions for master's pre-evaluation.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2 or higher) or Medium of Instruction (MOI) certificate
required_documents.notes: Required to satisfy the B2 English requirement; candidates without an accepted proof are assessed during the readiness test.

required_documents.document_name: Declaration of Value / CIMEA Statement of Comparability and legalization of the foreign qualification
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU visa applicants with a foreign qualification to finalise enrolment; obtained via the Italian Embassy/Consulate or CIMEA.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment after UniGeApply eligibility, then apply for the study visa.

source_quotes.quote: SAFE TRANSPORT AND LOGISTICS ENGINEERING, Course Code 11920, Degree Class LM-26 R (Safety Engineering), Teaching Language English, Campus Genova, 120 CFU, 2 years
This degree course is free access, but requires a test to be passed.

Bachelor's degree (of at least 180 CFU/ECTS) ... at least 36 CFU/ECTS or equivalent knowledge in the basic educational activities ... at least 45 CFU or equivalent knowledge in the characterizing activities for classes L-7, L-8, L-9. All the Bachelors in Engineering provided by Italian Universities meet the curricular requirements.
Students with an Italian bachelor's degree and a final mark of at least 9/10 ... are automatically verified as meeting adequacy requirements and skip the test.

Adequate knowledge of the English language equivalent at least to the B2 level ... certification (listed at clat.unige.it/en/CertificazioniRiconosciute) ... University Language Centre B2 test ... a degree taught entirely in English; if none apply, language knowledge must be verified during the personal preparation test.
Five scheduled sessions: September 8 & 28, November 18, January 18, and February 26.

uncertainty_notes: The corsi.unige.it deadlines page lists the generic UniGe enrolment calendar (pre-registration, confirmation, post-first-year and transfers) and does not separate EU vs non-EU first-year application windows; the institution-wide UniGeApply spring (residents) and fall (visa) windows were used to fill those.
No programme-specific fall-session early closure is published for Safe Transport and Logistics Engineering for A.Y. 2026/27.
Test session dates are given on the requisites page as day/month without explicit years; mapped to A.Y. 2026/27 (Sep/Nov 2026 and Jan/Feb 2027) consistent with the academic calendar.
The CLAT certificate-list URL was reported in two forms; the English path (clat.unige.it/en/CertificazioniRiconosciute) is used here.
